_meta.source: mortgagecalculator.org 5/1 ARM calculator article, 'Payment Comparison Table' worked example.
substitution_note: Bankrate's own ARM calculator (https://www.bankrate.com/mortgages/adjustable-rate-mortgage-calculator/) is JS-rendered; WebFetch returned the landing-page copy with no calculator output. We substituted with mortgagecalculator.org which publishes the same kind of worked example as static HTML (loan amount + initial rate + lifetime cap + per-year payment table).

The captured page documents a 5/1 ARM scenario: $240,000 / 30yr / initial 3.37% with a 5pp lifetime cap (max APR = 8.37%); the year-by-year amortization table on the page shows years 1-5 paying $1,060.37/mo at 3.37% and year 9+ paying $1,686.34/mo at 8.37% (lifetime-cap-bound).

Years 6-8 of the captured table use an unstated reset path (the page does not disclose the periodic cap explicitly), so we cross-validate only the year-1 P&I (cent-equal) and the year-5 ending balance (within $1, since the oracle's balance figures derive from rounded payments).

expected.year5_balance_note: Oracle reports $214,788.87 at end of year 5; engine reports $214,788.68. Drift of $0.19 is consistent with the oracle quoting a rounded payment ($1,060.37) and amortizing from there.
